zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Webb於1510年5月11日出生在英國瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃時，他的父親John Alexander Webb III27歲，母親Margaret Arden Dame of Parkhall41歲。他於1533年與Grace Arden在英國瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1554年3月3日在他的家鄉享年43歲，埋葬在英國維爾爾索爾茲伯里。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Webb于1510年5月11日出生在英国瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃时，他的父亲John Alexander Webb III27岁，母亲Margaret Arden Dame of Parkhall41岁。他于1533年与Grace Arden在英国瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1554年3月3日在他的家乡享年43岁，埋葬在英国維爾爾索爾茲伯里。

nameMeanings.name: Webb
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a weaver, from early Middle English webbe (Old English webba (masculine) or webbe (feminine), probably used of both male and female weavers). This word survived into Middle English long enough to give rise to the surname, but was already obsolescent as an agent noun; hence the secondary forms with the agent suffixes -er and -ster (see Webster , Webber and compare Weaver ).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, cognates of 1, including Weber and Weberman.
History: Richard Webb, a Lowland Scot, was an admitted freeman of Boston in 1632, and in 1635 was one of the first settlers of Hartford, CT.

variants.name: Webster
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webbester ‘weaver’ (Old English webbestre ‘female weaver’). By the time of surname formation, the gender distinction of the -stre suffix had almost completely disappeared. Compare Webb , Webber , and Weaver .

History: The name Webster was brought to North America from England independently by several different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One John Webster settled in Ipswich, MA, in 1635; another John Webster (died 1661), ancestor of the lexicographer Noah Webster, emigrated to Cambridge, MA, c. 1631 and later became one of the founders of the colony of CT, of which he was appointed governor in 1656. Daniel Webster (1782–1852), politician and orator, was born in Salisbury, NH, a descendant of Thomas Webster, a prominent 17th-century citizen of Ipswich, MA, whose family had settled there around 1635, while he was still a child.

variants.name: Webber
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webber, an agent derivative of Webb .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Weber   ‘weaver’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Webb was born on 11 May 1510, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England, his father, John Alexander Webb III, was 27 and his mother, Margaret Arden Dame of Parkhall, was 41. He married Grace Arden in 1533, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 3 March 1554, in his hometown, at the age of 43, and was buried in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England.

Il a épousé Grace Arden en 1533, à Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 3 mars 1554, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Webbが1510年5月11日にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストラトフォード オン アボンで生まれた時，父John Alexander Webb IIIは27歳，母Margaret Arden Dame of Parkhallは41歳であった。1533年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストラトフォード オン アボンでGrace Ardenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1554年3月3日に故郷で43歳で死去し，イギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Webb(이)가 1510년 5월 11일 영국 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Alexander Webb III(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Arden Dame of Parkhall(은)는 41세였다. 그는 1533년 영국 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 Grace Arden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1554년 3월 3일 그의 고향에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 윌츠히르 살리스버이에 묻혔다.

ru.Stratford-upon-Avon, Warwickshire, Engla: стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Webb родился 11 мая 1510 г. в местности стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Alexander Webb III было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Arden Dame of Parkhall, 41. Он женился на женщине по имени Grace Arden в 1533 г. в местности стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 3 марта 1554 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия.

es.11 May 1510–3 March 1554: 11 de mayo de 1510–3 de marzo de 1554

es.Stratford-upon-Avon, Warwickshire, Engla: Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Webb nació el 11 de mayo de 1510, en Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Alexander Webb III, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Margaret Arden Dame of Parkhall, tenía 41 años. Se casó con Grace Arden en 1533, en Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 3 de marzo de 1554, en su pueblo, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.
